title: Today's Tech Brief: 6 Best Prepaid Phone Plans (2026): Tello, Boost, Google Fi, More

body.text: I'm taking a look at the prepaid phone plan landscape, and it's clear that consumers are driving change. With the rise of affordable, flexible plans, it's no wonder that more people are ditching traditional contracts. According to Wired's latest roundup, there are some standout options for 2026. For instance, the best prepaid phone plan overall is US Mobile, which offers a range of customizable plans.

Tello, Boost, and Google Fi are among the top providers offering competitive prepaid plans. Tello's plans start at just $5 per month, which is pretty crazy - I mean, who doesn't love a good $5 plan? Boost offers a range of options, including an unlimited data plan for $50 per month. Google Fi's flexible plan, which charges users only for the data they use, is also worth considering. I like that these providers are shaking things up and making phone plans more affordable.

Another interesting development is AT&T's unlimited annual prepaid option. This kind of plan is great for those who want to avoid the hassle of monthly payments and commitments. And let's not forget about US Mobile's prepaid plans, which offer a lot of customization options. With so many choices out there, it's no wonder that prepaid is becoming increasingly popular.

6 Best Prepaid Phone Plans (2026): Tello, Boost, Google Fi, More is also worth a quick mention.
